name: test-driven-development
description: Use when implementing any feature or bugfix, before writing implementation code
---

# Test-Driven Development (TDD)

## Overview

Write the test first. Watch it fail. Write minimal code to pass.

**Core principle:** If you didn't watch the test fail, you don't know if it tests the right thing.

**Violating the letter of the rules is violating the spirit of the rules.**

## When to Use

**Always:**
- New features
- Bug fixes
- Refactoring
- Behavior changes

**Exceptions (ask your human partner):**
- Throwaway prototypes
- Generated code
- Configuration files

Thinking "skip TDD just this once"? Stop. That's rationalization.

## The Iron Law

```
NO PRODUCTION CODE WITHOUT A FAILING TEST FIRST
```

Write code before the test? Delete it. Start over.

**No exceptions:**
- Don't keep it as "reference"
- Don't "adapt" it while writing tests
- Don't look at it
- Delete means delete

Implement fresh from tests. Period.

## Red-Green-Refactor

```dot
digraph tdd_cycle {
    rankdir=LR;
    red [label="RED\nWrite failing test", shape=box, style=filled, fillcolor="#ffcccc"];
    verify_red [label="Verify fails\ncorrectly", shape=diamond];
    green [label="GREEN\nMinimal code", shape=box, style=filled, fillcolor="#ccffcc"];
    verify_green [label="Verify passes\nAll green", shape=diamond];
    refactor [label="REFACTOR\nClean up", shape=box, style=filled, fillcolor="#ccccff"];
    next [label="Next", shape=ellipse];

    red -> verify_red;
    verify_red -> green [label="yes"];
    verify_red -> red [label="wrong\nfailure"];
    green -> verify_green;
    verify_green -> refactor [label="yes"];
    verify_green -> green [label="no"];
    refactor -> verify_green [label="stay\ngreen"];
    verify_green -> next;
    next -> red;
}
```

### RED - Write Failing Test

Write one minimal test showing what should happen.

<Good>
```typescript
test('retries failed operations 3 times', async () => {
  let attempts = 0;
  const operation = () => {
    attempts++;
    if (attempts < 3) throw new Error('fail');
    return 'success';
  };

  const result = await retryOperation(operation);

  expect(result).toBe('success');
  expect(attempts).toBe(3);
});
```
Clear name, tests real behavior, one thing
</Good>

<Bad>
```typescript
test('retry works', async () => {
  const mock = jest.fn()
    .mockRejectedValueOnce(new Error())
    .mockRejectedValueOnce(new Error())
    .mockResolvedValueOnce('success');
  await retryOperation(mock);
  expect(mock).toHaveBeenCalledTimes(3);
});
```
Vague name, tests mock not code
</Bad>

**Requirements:**
- One behavior
- Clear name
- Real code (no mocks unless unavoidable)

### Verify RED - Watch It Fail

**MANDATORY. Never skip.**

```bash
npm test path/to/test.test.ts
```

Confirm:
- Test fails (not errors)
- Failure message is expected
- Fails because feature missing (not typos)

**Test passes?** You're testing existing behavior. Fix test.

**Test errors?** Fix error, re-run until it fails correctly.

### GREEN - Minimal Code

Write simplest code to pass the test.

<Good>
```typescript
async function retryOperation<T>(fn: () => Promise<T>): Promise<T> {
  for (let i = 0; i < 3; i++) {
    try {
      return await fn();
    } catch (e) {
      if (i === 2) throw e;
    }
  }
  throw new Error('unreachable');
}
```
Just enough to pass
</Good>

<Bad>
```typescript
async function retryOperation<T>(
  fn: () => Promise<T>,
  options?: {
    maxRetries?: number;
    backoff?: 'linear' | 'exponential';
    onRetry?: (attempt: number) => void;
  }
): Promise<T> {
  // YAGNI
}
```
Over-engineered
</Bad>

Don't add features, refactor other code, or "improve" beyond the test.

### Verify GREEN - Watch It Pass

**MANDATORY.**

```bash
npm test path/to/test.test.ts
```

Confirm:
- Test passes
- Other tests still pass
- Output pristine (no errors, warnings)

**Test fails?** Fix code, not test.

**Other tests fail?** Fix now.

### REFACTOR - Clean Up

After green only:
- Remove duplication
- Improve names
- Extract helpers

Keep tests green. Don't add behavior.

### Repeat

Next failing test for next feature.

## Good Tests

| Quality | Good | Bad |
|---------|------|-----|
| **Minimal** | One thing. "and" in name? Split it. | `test('validates email and domain and whitespace')` |
| **Clear** | Name describes behavior | `test('test1')` |
| **Shows intent** | Demonstrates desired API | Obscures what code should do |

## Why Order Matters

**"I'll write tests after to verify it works"**

Tests written after code pass immediately. Passing immediately proves nothing:
- Might test wrong thing
- Might test implementation, not behavior
- Might miss edge cases you forgot
- You never saw it catch the bug

Test-first forces you to see the test fail, proving it actually tests something.

**"I already manually tested all the edge cases"**

Manual testing is ad-hoc. You think you tested everything but:
- No record of what you tested
- Can't re-run when code changes
- Easy to forget cases under pressure
- "It worked when I tried it" 鈮?comprehensive

Automated tests are systematic. They run the same way every time.

**"Deleting X hours of work is wasteful"**

Sunk cost fallacy. The time is already gone. Your choice now:
- Delete and rewrite with TDD (X more hours, high confidence)
- Keep it and add tests after (30 min, low confidence, likely bugs)

The "waste" is keeping code you can't trust. Working code without real tests is technical debt.

**"TDD is dogmatic, being pragmatic means adapting"**

TDD IS pragmatic:
- Finds bugs before commit (faster than debugging after)
- Prevents regressions (tests catch breaks immediately)
- Documents behavior (tests show how to use code)
- Enables refactoring (change freely, tests catch breaks)

"Pragmatic" shortcuts = debugging in production = slower.

**"Tests after achieve the same goals - it's spirit not ritual"**

No. Tests-after answer "What does this do?" Tests-first answer "What should this do?"

Tests-after are biased by your implementation. You test what you built, not what's required. You verify remembered edge cases, not discovered ones.

Tests-first force edge case discovery before implementing. Tests-after verify you remembered everything (you didn't).

30 minutes of tests after 鈮?TDD. You get coverage, lose proof tests work.

## Common Rationalizations

| Excuse | Reality |
|--------|---------|
| "Too simple to test" | Simple code breaks. Test takes 30 seconds. |
| "I'll test after" | Tests passing immediately prove nothing. |
| "Tests after achieve same goals" | Tests-after = "what does this do?" Tests-first = "what should this do?" |
| "Already manually tested" | Ad-hoc 鈮?systematic. No record, can't re-run. |
| "Deleting X hours is wasteful" | Sunk cost fallacy. Keeping unverified code is technical debt. |
| "Keep as reference, write tests first" | You'll adapt it. That's testing after. Delete means delete. |
| "Need to explore first" | Fine. Throw away exploration, start with TDD. |
| "Test hard = design unclear" | Listen to test. Hard to test = hard to use. |
| "TDD will slow me down" | TDD faster than debugging. Pragmatic = test-first. |
| "Manual test faster" | Manual doesn't prove edge cases. You'll re-test every change. |
| "Existing code has no tests" | You're improving it. Add tests for existing code. |

## Red Flags - STOP and Start Over

- Code before test
- Test after implementation
- Test passes immediately
- Can't explain why test failed
- Tests added "later"
- Rationalizing "just this once"
- "I already manually tested it"
- "Tests after achieve the same purpose"
- "It's about spirit not ritual"
- "Keep as reference" or "adapt existing code"
- "Already spent X hours, deleting is wasteful"
- "TDD is dogmatic, I'm being pragmatic"
- "This is different because..."

**All of these mean: Delete code. Start over with TDD.**

## Example: Bug Fix

**Bug:** Empty email accepted

**RED**
```typescript
test('rejects empty email', async () => {
  const result = await submitForm({ email: '' });
  expect(result.error).toBe('Email required');
});
```

**Verify RED**
```bash
$ npm test
FAIL: expected 'Email required', got undefined
```

**GREEN**
```typescript
function submitForm(data: FormData) {
  if (!data.email?.trim()) {
    return { error: 'Email required' };
  }
  // ...
}
```

**Verify GREEN**
```bash
$ npm test
PASS
```

**REFACTOR**
Extract validation for multiple fields if needed.

## Verification Checklist

Before marking work complete:

- [ ] Every new function/method has a test
- [ ] Watched each test fail before implementing
- [ ] Each test failed for expected reason (feature missing, not typo)
- [ ] Wrote minimal code to pass each test
- [ ] All tests pass
- [ ] Output pristine (no errors, warnings)
- [ ] Tests use real code (mocks only if unavoidable)
- [ ] Edge cases and errors covered

Can't check all boxes? You skipped TDD. Start over.

## When Stuck

| Problem | Solution |
|---------|----------|
| Don't know how to test | Write wished-for API. Write assertion first. Ask your human partner. |
| Test too complicated | Design too complicated. Simplify interface. |
| Must mock everything | Code too coupled. Use dependency injection. |
| Test setup huge | Extract helpers. Still complex? Simplify design. |

## Debugging Integration

Bug found? Write failing test reproducing it. Follow TDD cycle. Test proves fix and prevents regression.

Never fix bugs without a test.

## Testing Anti-Patterns

When adding mocks or test utilities, read @testing-anti-patterns.md to avoid common pitfalls:
- Testing mock behavior instead of real behavior
- Adding test-only methods to production classes
- Mocking without understanding dependencies

## Final Rule

```
Production code 鈫?test exists and failed first
Otherwise 鈫?not TDD
```

No exceptions without your human partner's permission.

Wall time: 0.4 seconds

---
name: verification-before-completion
description: Use when about to claim work is complete, fixed, or passing, before committing or creating PRs - requires running verification commands and confirming output before making any success claims; evidence before assertions always
---

# Verification Before Completion

## Overview

Claiming work is complete without verification is dishonesty, not efficiency.

**Core principle:** Evidence before claims, always.

**Violating the letter of this rule is violating the spirit of this rule.**

## The Iron Law

```
NO COMPLETION CLAIMS WITHOUT FRESH VERIFICATION EVIDENCE
```

If you haven't run the verification command in this message, you cannot claim it passes.

## The Gate Function

```
BEFORE claiming any status or expressing satisfaction:

1. IDENTIFY: What command proves this claim?
2. RUN: Execute the FULL command (fresh, complete)
3. READ: Full output, check exit code, count failures
4. VERIFY: Does output confirm the claim?
   - If NO: State actual status with evidence
   - If YES: State claim WITH evidence
5. ONLY THEN: Make the claim

Skip any step = lying, not verifying
```

## Common Failures

| Claim | Requires | Not Sufficient |
|-------|----------|----------------|
| Tests pass | Test command output: 0 failures | Previous run, "should pass" |
| Linter clean | Linter output: 0 errors | Partial check, extrapolation |
| Build succeeds | Build command: exit 0 | Linter passing, logs look good |
| Bug fixed | Test original symptom: passes | Code changed, assumed fixed |
| Regression test works | Red-green cycle verified | Test passes once |
| Agent completed | VCS diff shows changes | Agent reports "success" |
| Requirements met | Line-by-line checklist | Tests passing |

## Red Flags - STOP

- Using "should", "probably", "seems to"
- Expressing satisfaction before verification ("Great!", "Perfect!", "Done!", etc.)
- About to commit/push/PR without verification
- Trusting agent success reports
- Relying on partial verification
- Thinking "just this once"
- Tired and wanting work over
- **ANY wording implying success without having run verification**

## Rationalization Prevention

| Excuse | Reality |
|--------|---------|
| "Should work now" | RUN the verification |
| "I'm confident" | Confidence 鈮?evidence |
| "Just this once" | No exceptions |
| "Linter passed" | Linter 鈮?compiler |
| "Agent said success" | Verify independently |
| "I'm tired" | Exhaustion 鈮?excuse |
| "Partial check is enough" | Partial proves nothing |
| "Different words so rule doesn't apply" | Spirit over letter |

## Key Patterns

**Tests:**
```
鉁?[Run test command] [See: 34/34 pass] "All tests pass"
鉂?"Should pass now" / "Looks correct"
```

**Regression tests (TDD Red-Green):**
```
鉁?Write 鈫?Run (pass) 鈫?Revert fix 鈫?Run (MUST FAIL) 鈫?Restore 鈫?Run (pass)
鉂?"I've written a regression test" (without red-green verification)
```

**Build:**
```
鉁?[Run build] [See: exit 0] "Build passes"
鉂?"Linter passed" (linter doesn't check compilation)
```

**Requirements:**
```
鉁?Re-read plan 鈫?Create checklist 鈫?Verify each 鈫?Report gaps or completion
鉂?"Tests pass, phase complete"
```

**Agent delegation:**
```
鉁?Agent reports success 鈫?Check VCS diff 鈫?Verify changes 鈫?Report actual state
鉂?Trust agent report
```

## Why This Matters

From 24 failure memories:
- your human partner said "I don't believe you" - trust broken
- Undefined functions shipped - would crash
- Missing requirements shipped - incomplete features
- Time wasted on false completion 鈫?redirect 鈫?rework
- Violates: "Honesty is a core value. If you lie, you'll be replaced."

## When To Apply

**ALWAYS before:**
- ANY variation of success/completion claims
- ANY expression of satisfaction
- ANY positive statement about work state
- Committing, PR creation, task completion
- Moving to next task
- Delegating to agents

**Rule applies to:**
- Exact phrases
- Paraphrases and synonyms
- Implications of success
- ANY communication suggesting completion/correctness

## The Bottom Line

**No shortcuts for verification.**

Run the command. Read the output. THEN claim the result.

This is non-negotiable.

# Executing Plans

## Overview

Load plan, review critically, execute all tasks, report when complete.

**Announce at start:** "I'm using the executing-plans skill to implement this plan."

**Note:** Tell your human partner that Superpowers works much better with access to subagents. The quality of its work will be significantly higher if run on a platform with subagent support (such as Claude Code or Codex). If subagents are available, use superpowers:subagent-driven-development instead of this skill.

## The Process

### Step 1: Load and Review Plan
1. Read plan file
2. Review critically - identify any questions or concerns about the plan
3. If concerns: Raise them with your human partner before starting
4. If no concerns: Create TodoWrite and proceed

### Step 2: Execute Tasks

For each task:
1. Mark as in_progress
2. Follow each step exactly (plan has bite-sized steps)
3. Run verifications as specified
4. Mark as completed

### Step 3: Complete Development

After all tasks complete and verified:
- Announce: "I'm using the finishing-a-development-branch skill to complete this work."
- **REQUIRED SUB-SKILL:** Use superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch
- Follow that skill to verify tests, present options, execute choice

## When to Stop and Ask for Help

**STOP executing immediately when:**
- Hit a blocker (missing dependency, test fails, instruction unclear)
- Plan has critical gaps preventing starting
- You don't understand an instruction
- Verification fails repeatedly

**Ask for clarification rather than guessing.**

## When to Revisit Earlier Steps

**Return to Review (Step 1) when:**
- Partner updates the plan based on your feedback
- Fundamental approach needs rethinking

**Don't force through blockers** - stop and ask.

## Remember
- Review plan critically first
- Follow plan steps exactly
- Don't skip verifications
- Reference skills when plan says to
- Stop when blocked, don't guess
- Never start implementation on main/master branch without explicit user consent

## Integration

**Required workflow skills:**
- **superpowers:using-git-worktrees** - REQUIRED: Set up isolated workspace before starting
- **superpowers:writing-plans** - Creates the plan this skill executes
- **superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch** - Complete development after all tasks

shell_command output=Exit code: 0 Wall time: 0.4 seconds Output: default_voice=os.getenv("CLONECUT_TTS_DEFAULT_VOICE", tts_default_voice), sample_rate_hz=tts_local_sample_rate_value, logger=self.log...
Show output payload 
Exit code: 0
Wall time: 0.4 seconds
Output:
            default_voice=os.getenv("CLONECUT_TTS_DEFAULT_VOICE", tts_default_voice),
            sample_rate_hz=tts_local_sample_rate_value,
            logger=self.logger,
        )
        self.tts_http_adapter = HttpTtsAdapter(
            enabled=self.tts_adapter_enabled,
            default_voice=os.getenv("CLONECUT_TTS_DEFAULT_VOICE", tts_default_voice),
            api_endpoint=os.getenv("CLONECUT_TTS_API_ENDPOINT", tts_api_endpoint),
            api_timeout_seconds=float(
                os.getenv("CLONECUT_TTS_API_TIMEOUT_SECONDS", str(tts_api_timeout))
            ),
            logger=self.logger,
        )

        video_cmd = str(self._get_worker_cfg("video_command_template", ""))
        video_timeout_raw = self._get_worker_cfg("video_command_timeout_seconds", 7200)
        try:
            video_timeout = int(video_timeout_raw)
        except Exception:
            video_timeout = 7200
        self.video_adapter = ExternalVideoCommandAdapter(
            output_dir=self.config.output_dir,
            command_template=os.getenv("CLONECUT_VIDEO_COMMAND_TEMPLATE", video_cmd),
            timeout_seconds=int(
                os.getenv("CLONECUT_VIDEO_COMMAND_TIMEOUT_SECONDS", str(video_timeout))
            ),
            logger=self.logger,
        )
        self.internal_pipeline_enabled = self._bool_cfg(
            os.getenv("CLONECUT_USE_INTERNAL_PIPELINE"),
            bool(self._get_worker_cfg("use_internal_pipeline", False)),
        )

        # --- telemetry ---
        telemetry_interval = float(self._get_worker_cfg("resource_monitor_interval_seconds", 5.0))
        report_dir = str(self._get_worker_cfg("performance_report_dir", "logs/performance_reports"))
        self.resource_monitor = ResourceMonitor(
            interval_seconds=telemetry_interval,
            disk_path=str(self.config.output_dir),
            logger=self.logger,
        )
        self.performance_tracker = PerformanceTracker(resource_monitor=self.resource_monitor)
        self.report_generator = ReportGenerator(report_dir=report_dir, logger=self.logger)

    def start(self) -> None:
        if any(thread.is_alive() for thread in self.worker_threads):
            return

        self.resource_monitor.start()
        recovered = self._recover_stuck_tasks()
        restored_retries = self._restore_retry_schedule()
        self.stop_event.clear()
        self.worker_threads = []
        self._spawn_workers("download", self.max_download_workers, self._download_loop)
        self._spawn_workers("process", self.max_process_workers, self._process_loop)
        self._spawn_workers("upload", self.max_upload_workers, self._upload_loop)
        self._spawn_workers("retry", 1, self._retry_scheduler_loop)
        self._spawn_workers("zombie", 1, self._zombie_cleaner_loop)

        self.logger.info(
            "worker adapters: tts_enabled=%s tts_engine=%s tts_http_ready=%s video_cmd_enabled=%s internal_pipeline=%s redis_registry=%s redis_queue=%s recovered=%s restored_retries=%s",
            self.tts_adapter_enabled,
            self.tts_engine_mode,
            self.tts_http_adapter.is_ready(),
            self.video_adapter.is_enabled(),
            self.internal_pipeline_enabled,
            self.registry.is_persistent(),
            self.use_redis_queue,
            recovered,
            restored_retries,
        )
        self.logger.info(
            "worker pools started: download=%s process=%s upload=%s retry_scheduler=%s zombie_timeout=%ss zombie_by_status=%s video_pipeline_path=%s",
            self.max_download_workers,
            self.max_process_workers,
            self.max_upload_workers,
            1,
            self.zombie_timeout_seconds,
            self.zombie_timeout_by_status_seconds,
            "direct",
        )

    def stop(self) -> None:
        self.stop_event.set()
        for thread in self.worker_threads:
            thread.join(timeout=2.0)
        self.resource_monitor.stop()
        self.logger.info("worker service stopped")

    def submit(self, raw_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> Tuple[bool, str, Optional[str]]:
        try:
            task = self._build_task(raw_data)
        except ValueError as exc:
            return False, "", str(exc)

        existing_task_id = self.queue_adapter.check_duplicate(task)
        if existing_task_id:
            self.logger.info(
                "duplicate task ignored: incoming=%s existing=%s",
                task.task_id,
                existing_task_id,
            )
            return True, existing_task_id, None

        self._persist_task(task)
        self.queue_adapter.remember_dedup(task, ttl_seconds=self.dedup_ttl_seconds)
        self._enqueue_task(self._initial_queue_for_task(task), task)

        with self.lock:
            self.stats["received"] += 1

        self.logger.info(
            "task received: task_id=%s type=%s queued=%s",
            task.task_id,
            task.task_type.value,
            self._initial_queue_for_task(task),
        )
        return True, task.task_id, None

    def get_status(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        queue_sizes = self.queue_adapter.get_queue_sizes()
        queued_total = (
            int(queue_sizes.get("download", 0))
            + int(queue_sizes.get("process", 0))
            + int(queue_sizes.get("upload", 0))
        )
        task_status_counts = self.get_task_status_counts()

        with self.lock:
            running = sum(self.active_workers.values())
            stats = dict(self.stats)
            active_workers = dict(self.active_workers)
        active_task_count = (
            int(task_status_counts.get(TaskStatus.PENDING.value, 0))
            + int(task_status_counts.get(TaskStatus.ASSIGNED.value, 0))
            + int(task_status_counts.get(TaskStatus.DOWNLOADING.value, 0))
            + int(task_status_counts.get(TaskStatus.PROCESSING.value, 0))
            + int(task_status_counts.get(TaskStatus.UPLOADING.value, 0))
        )
        status_metrics = {
            "total": int(task_status_counts.get("total", 0)),
Tool Result #184 shell_command call_WYbtTJBGIhjC1VVEIo6EPnfT exit 0 10:30:51
shell_command output=Exit code: 0 Wall time: 0.4 seconds Output: raise RuntimeError("no video engine configured") def _run_internal_video_pipeline(self, task: UnifiedTask, source_path: str) -> Dict[...
Show output payload 
Exit code: 0
Wall time: 0.4 seconds
Output:
        raise RuntimeError("no video engine configured")

    def _run_internal_video_pipeline(self, task: UnifiedTask, source_path: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        task_dir = Path(self.config.output_dir) / task.task_id
        task_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        source = Path(source_path)
        if not source.exists():
            task.stage_checkpoint = "stage0_clean"
            task.current_stage = 0
            raise RuntimeError(f"source file not found: {source}")
        stage_names = list(INTERNAL_PIPELINE_STAGE_ORDER)
        start_from_stage = 0
        self._prepare_internal_pipeline_context(task=task, task_dir=task_dir)

        if self._should_use_internal_pipeline_migration_fallback(source):
            return self._run_internal_video_pipeline_fallback_copy(
                task=task,
                source=source,
                task_dir=task_dir,
                stage_names=stage_names,
                start_from_stage=start_from_stage,
                reason="migration fallback: tiny input",
            )

        pipeline = VideoPipeline(
            config=VideoPipelineConfig.from_env(output_dir=str(self.config.output_dir)),
            logger=self.logger,
            performance_tracker=self.performance_tracker,
            resource_monitor=self.resource_monitor,
        )
        started = time.perf_counter()
        pipeline_result = pipeline.run(
            input_video=str(source),
            task_id=task.task_id,
            start_from_stage=start_from_stage,
            forum_post_info=dict(task.metadata.get("forum_post_info") or {})
            if isinstance(task.metadata, dict)
            else {},
        )
        elapsed_ms = int(max(0.0, (time.perf_counter() - started) * 1000))

        last_stage = str(getattr(pipeline_result, "last_stage", "") or "").strip()
        failed_stage = str(getattr(pipeline_result, "failed_stage", "") or "").strip()
        checkpoint = str(last_stage or failed_stage).strip()
        if checkpoint:
            task.stage_checkpoint = checkpoint
        task.current_stage = int(getattr(pipeline_result, "current_stage", start_from_stage))
        succeeded = bool(getattr(pipeline_result, "success", False))

        output_path = str(getattr(pipeline_result, "output_file", "") or "").strip()
        self._write_internal_pipeline_state(
            task_dir=task_dir,
            output_path=output_path,
            stage_name=checkpoint,
            current_stage=task.current_stage,
            failed_stage=failed_stage,
            succeeded=succeeded,
        )

        if not succeeded:
            error_message = str(getattr(pipeline_result, "error_message", "") or "").strip()
            raise RuntimeError(error_message or "video pipeline failed")

        return {
            "success": True,
            "request_type": task.task_type.value,
            "output_path": output_path,
            "executed_stages": list(getattr(pipeline_result, "executed_stages", []) or []),
            "executed_mode": "video_pipeline",
            "performance_summary": (
                str(getattr(pipeline_result, "performance_summary", "") or "").strip()
                or f"video pipeline executed duration_ms={elapsed_ms}"
            ),
        }

    def _should_use_internal_pipeline_migration_fallback(self, source: Path) -> bool:
        threshold_raw = self._get_worker_cfg("internal_pipeline_migration_fallback_max_bytes", 0)
        try:
            threshold = max(0, int(threshold_raw))
        except Exception:
            threshold = 0
        if threshold <= 0:
            return False
        try:
            return source.stat().st_size <= threshold
        except Exception:
            return False

    def _write_internal_pipeline_state(
        self,
        *,
        task_dir: Path,
        output_path: str,
        stage_name: str,
        current_stage: int,
        failed_stage: str = "",
        succeeded: bool = True,
    ) -> None:
        last_successful_stage = self._resolve_internal_pipeline_last_successful_stage_name(
            stage_name=stage_name,
            failed_stage=failed_stage if not succeeded else "",
            current_stage=current_stage,
        )
        completed_step, completed_stage_name = INTERNAL_PIPELINE_COMPLETION_BY_STAGE.get(
            last_successful_stage,
            (-1, ""),
Tool Result #185 shell_command call_4BqgSCNh6MLGaSzlsChSdQEe exit 0 10:30:51

Show output payload 
Exit code: 0
Wall time: 0.4 seconds
Output:
            merged["speaker"] = speaker

        original_filename = self._resolve_original_filename(
            forum_post_info=merged,
            payload=payload,
            metadata=metadata,
            source_url="",
        )
        if original_filename:
            merged["original_filename"] = original_filename
        return merged

    def _normalize_text_list(self, *values: Any) -> List[str]:
        output: List[str] = []
        seen: set[str] = set()
        for value in values:
            items: List[str] = []
            if isinstance(value, list):
                items = [str(item or "").strip() for item in value]
            elif isinstance(value, str):
                items = [item.strip() for item in value.replace("\uFF0C", ",").split(",")]
            for item in items:
                normalized = " ".join(item.split())
                if not normalized:
                    continue
                dedup_key = normalized.lower()
                if dedup_key in seen:
                    continue
                seen.add(dedup_key)
                output.append(normalized)
        return output

    def _pick_first_text(self, *values: Any) -> str:
        for value in values:
            text = str(value or "").strip()
            if text:
                return text
        return ""

    def _sanitize_filename(self, value: str) -> str:
        text = str(value or "").strip()
        if not text:
            return ""
        text = text.replace("\\", "/").split("/")[-1].strip()
        text = re.sub(r"[\x00-\x1f]", "", text)
        text = re.sub(r"[<>:\"/\\\\|?*]+", "_", text)
        text = re.sub(r"\s+", " ", text).strip().strip(".")
        return text

    def _init_task_storage(self) -> Optional[SqliteStorageAdapter]:
        db_path = os.getenv("CLONECUT_DB_PATH", "data/clonecut.db")
        try:
            return SqliteStorageAdapter(db_path=db_path, logger=self.logger)
        except Exception as exc:
            self.logger.warning("sqlite storage disabled: %s", exc)
            return None

    def _save_task_snapshot(self, task: UnifiedTask) -> None:
        self._sync_task_metadata(task)
        if self.task_storage is None:
            return
        self.task_storage.save_task(task)

    def _persist_task(self, task: UnifiedTask) -> None:
        self._sync_task_metadata(task)
        self.registry.add(task)
        self._save_task_snapshot(task)

    def _sync_task_metadata(self, task: UnifiedTask) -> None:
        metadata = dict(task.metadata or {})
        metadata["current_stage"] = int(task.current_stage)
        metadata["max_retries"] = int(task.max_retries)
        metadata["retry_count"] = int(task.retry_count)
        if task.stage_checkpoint:
            metadata["stage_checkpoint"] = task.stage_checkpoint
        else:
            metadata.pop("stage_checkpoint", None)
        task.metadata = metadata

    def _transition_status(self, task: UnifiedTask, status: TaskStatus, strict: bool = True) -> bool:
        result = self.registry.update_status(task.task_id, status)
        if result.ok:
            task.status = status
            if status in {TaskStatus.PENDING, TaskStatus.FAILED, TaskStatus.CANCELLED}:
                task.started_at = None
            return True
        if strict:
            raise RuntimeError(result.reason or f"status transition failed: {task.task_id}")
        return False

    def _mark_task_active(self, task_id: str, active: bool) -> None:
        with self.lock:
            if active:
                self.active_task_ids.add(task_id)
            else:
                self.active_task_ids.discard(task_id)

    def _schedule_retry(self, task: UnifiedTask) -> bool:
        if task.status != TaskStatus.FAILED:
            return False
        retryable = bool((task.metadata or {}).get("retryable_failure", True))
        if not retryable:
            return False
        max_retries = max(0, int(task.max_retries))
        if task.retry_count >= max_retries:
            return False

        delay = self._retry_delay_seconds(task, task.retry_count)
        retry_count = task.retry_count + 1
        due_at = time.time() + delay
        task.retry_count = retry_count
        task.metadata = dict(task.metadata or {})
        task.metadata["retry_due_at"] = due_at
        task.metadata["retry_delay_seconds"] = delay
        self._persist_task(task)

        with self.lock:
            self.retry_index[task.task_id] = (due_at, task)
            heapq.heappush(self.retry_heap, (due_at, task.task_id))
            self.stats["retried"] += 1
        return True

    def _retry_delay_seconds(self, task: UnifiedTask, retry_count_before_increment: int) -> int:
        failed_from = str((task.metadata or {}).get("failed_from_status", "")).strip().lower()
        backoff = self.retry_backoff_seconds or [5]
        if failed_from == TaskStatus.UPLOADING.value:
            backoff = self.upload_retry_backoff_seconds or [1, 2, 4]
        idx = min(max(0, int(retry_count_before_increment)), len(backoff) - 1)
        return int(backoff[idx])

    def _is_retryable_failure(self, error: Exception, failed_from: TaskStatus) -> bool:
        if failed_from != TaskStatus.UPLOADING:
            return True
        if isinstance(error, RetryableUploadError):
            return True
        if isinstance(error, NonRetryableUploadError):
            return False
        return False

    def _remove_retry_schedule(self, task_id: str) -> None:
        with self.lock:
            self.retry_index.pop(task_id, None)

    def _retry_scheduler_loop(self) -> None:
        while not self.stop_event.is_set():
            ready = self._dequeue_ready_retries()
            for task in ready:
                self._enqueue_retry_task(task)
            self.stop_event.wait(self.retry_check_interval_seconds)

    def _dequeue_ready_retries(self) -> List[UnifiedTask]:
        now_ts = time.time()
        ready: List[UnifiedTask] = []
        with self.lock:
            while self.retry_heap:
                due_at, task_id = self.retry_heap[0]
                if due_at > now_ts:
                    break
                heapq.heappop(self.retry_heap)
                indexed = self.retry_index.get(task_id)
                if indexed is None:
                    continue
                idx_due_at, task = indexed
                if idx_due_at != due_at:
                    continue
                self.retry_index.pop(task_id, None)
                ready.append(task)
        return ready

    def _enqueue_retry_task(self, task: UnifiedTask) -> None:
        queue_name = self._queue_for_retry_task(task)
        task.metadata = dict(task.metadata or {})
        task.metadata.pop("retry_due_at", None)
        task.metadata.pop("retry_delay_seconds", None)
        task.metadata.pop("retryable_failure", None)
        task.error_message = None
        task.completed_at = None
        task.started_at = None
        transitioned = self._transition_status(task, TaskStatus.PENDING, strict=False)
        if not transitioned:
            task.status = TaskStatus.PENDING
            self._persist_task(task)
        else:
            self._persist_task(task)
        if queue_name == "upload":
            transitioned_upload = self._transition_status(task, TaskStatus.PROCESSING, strict=False)
            if not transitioned_upload:
                task.status = TaskStatus.PROCESSING
            self._persist_task(task)
        self._enqueue_task(queue_name, task)
        self.logger.info(
            "task retry enqueued: task_id=%s retry_count=%s/%s queue=%s",
            task.task_id,
            task.retry_count,
            task.max_retries,
            queue_name,
        )

    def _queue_for_retry_task(self, task: UnifiedTask) -> str:
        failed_from = str((task.metadata or {}).get("failed_from_status", "")).strip().lower()
        if failed_from == TaskStatus.UPLOADING.value and self._has_uploadable_output(task):
            return "upload"
        if failed_from == TaskStatus.DOWNLOADING.value and self._needs_download(task):
            return "download"
        if failed_from == TaskStatus.PROCESSING.value:
            return "process"

Output:
from __future__ import annotations

from dataclasses import dataclass, field
import logging
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any, Callable, Dict, List, Optional

from domain.video.config import VideoPipelineConfig
from domain.video.stages import (
    run_stage0_clean,
    run_stage1_transcode,
    run_stage2_silence,
    run_stage3_repair,
    run_stage4_asr,
    run_stage5_ai_text,
    run_stage6_5_resub,
    run_stage6_clip,
    run_stage7_correct,
    run_stage7_subtitle,
    run_stage8_cover,
    run_stage9_organize,
)
from domain.video.stages.base import StageResult
from domain.video.stages.utils import probe_duration_seconds


@dataclass
class PipelineResult:
    success: bool
    output_file: Optional[str] = None
    error_message: Optional[str] = None
    executed_stages: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    performance_summary: str = ""
    current_stage: int = 0
    last_stage: Optional[str] = None
    failed_stage: Optional[str] = None
    performance_report: Optional[Any] = None


class VideoPipeline:
    """Internal multi-stage video pipeline."""

    def __init__(
        self,
        config: VideoPipelineConfig,
        logger: Optional[logging.Logger] = None,
        performance_tracker: Any = None,
        resource_monitor: Any = None,
    ) -> None:
        self.config = config
        self.logger = logger or logging.getLogger("clonecut.domain.video.pipeline")
        self.performance_tracker = performance_tracker
        self.resource_monitor = resource_monitor
        self._stages: List[tuple[str, Callable[..., StageResult]]] = [
            ("stage0_clean", self._stage0),
            ("stage1_transcode", self._stage1),
            ("stage2_silence", self._stage2),
            ("stage3_repair", self._stage3),
            ("stage4_asr", self._stage4),
            ("stage5_ai_text", self._stage5),
            ("stage6_clip", self._stage6),
            ("stage6_5_resub", self._stage6_5),
            ("stage7_correct", self._stage7_correct),
            ("stage7_subtitle", self._stage7),
            ("stage8_cover", self._stage8),
            ("stage9_organize", self._stage9),
        ]

    def run(
        self,
        input_video: str,
        task_id: str,
        start_from_stage: int = 0,
        forum_post_info: Optional[dict] = None,
    ) -> PipelineResult:
        current = input_video
        executed: List[str] = []
        stage_start = max(0, int(start_from_stage))
        context: Dict[str, Any] = {"forum_post_info": _normalize_forum_post_info(forum_post_info)}
        last_stage_index = max(0, min(stage_start, len(self._stages) - 1 if self._stages else 0))
        last_stage_name: Optional[str] = None
        gpu_stage_indexes = {1, 2, 4, 6}
        tracking_started = False

        if self.performance_tracker is not None:
            try:
                path = Path(input_video)
                file_size_mb = 0.0
                if path.exists():
                    file_size_mb = path.stat().st_size / (1024 * 1024)
                duration_seconds = probe_duration_seconds(input_video)
                self.performance_tracker.start_video_processing(
                    task_id=task_id,
                    video_filename=path.name,
                    original_filename=path.name,
                    file_size_mb=file_size_mb,
                    video_duration_seconds=duration_seconds,
                )
                tracking_started = True
            except Exception as exc:
                self.logger.warning("start performance tracking failed: task=%s err=%s", task_id, exc)

        for idx, (stage_name, handler) in enumerate(self._stages):
            if idx < stage_start:
                continue

            if self.resource_monitor is not None:
                try:
                    self.resource_monitor.record_step_resource(
                        step_num=idx,
                        step_name=stage_name,
                        task_id=task_id,
                    )
                except Exception as exc:
                    self.logger.debug("resource snapshot failed: task=%s stage=%s err=%s", task_id, stage_name, exc)
            if self.performance_tracker is not None:
                try:
                    self.performance_tracker.start_stage(
                        task_id=task_id,
                        stage_name=stage_name,
                        gpu_accelerated=(idx in gpu_stage_indexes),
                    )
                except Exception:
                    pass
            try:
                result = handler(current, task_id=task_id, stage_name=stage_name, context=context)
            except Exception as exc:
                if self.performance_tracker is not None:
                    try:
                        self.performance_tracker.end_stage(
                            task_id=task_id,
                            stage_name=stage_name,
                            success=False,
                            error_message=str(exc),
                        )
                        self.performance_tracker.add_error(task_id, str(exc))
                    except Exception:
                        pass
                perf_report = self._finish_tracking(task_id, tracking_started)
                return PipelineResult(
                    success=False,
                    output_file=current,
                    error_message=f"{stage_name} exception: {exc}",
                    executed_stages=executed,
                    current_stage=idx,
                    last_stage=last_stage_name,
                    failed_stage=stage_name,
                    performance_report=perf_report,
                )

            executed.append(stage_name)
            last_stage_index = idx
            last_stage_name = stage_name
            if not result.success:
                if self.performance_tracker is not None:
                    try:
                        self.performance_tracker.end_stage(
                            task_id=task_id,
                            stage_name=stage_name,
                            success=False,
                            error_message=str(result.error_message or ""),
                        )
                        self.performance_tracker.add_error(
                            task_id,
                            str(result.error_message or f"{stage_name} failed"),
                        )
                    except Exception:
                        pass
                perf_report = self._finish_tracking(task_id, tracking_started)
                return PipelineResult(
                    success=False,
                    output_file=current,
                    error_message=result.error_message or f"{stage_name} failed",
                    executed_stages=executed,
                    current_stage=idx,
                    last_stage=last_stage_name,
                    failed_stage=stage_name,
                    performance_report=perf_report,
                )
            if self.performance_tracker is not None:
                try:
                    self.performance_tracker.end_stage(
                        task_id=task_id,
                        stage_name=stage_name,
                        success=True,
                    )
                except Exception:
                    pass
            if result.output_file:
                current = result.output_file

        perf_report = self._finish_tracking(task_id, tracking_started)
        summary = f"internal pipeline executed stages={len(executed)}"
        if perf_report is not None:
            try:
                ratio = float(getattr(perf_report, "processing_speed_ratio", 0.0))
                if ratio > 0:
                    summary = f"internal pipeline executed stages={len(executed)} speed={ratio:.2f}x"
            except Exception:
                pass
        return PipelineResult(
            success=True,
            output_file=current,
            executed_stages=executed,
            performance_summary=summary,
            current_stage=last_stage_index,
            last_stage=last_stage_name,
            performance_report=perf_report,
        )

    def _finish_tracking(self, task_id: str, tracking_started: bool) -> Optional[Any]:
        if not tracking_started or self.performance_tracker is None:
            return None
        try:
            return self.performance_tracker.end_video_processing(task_id)
        except Exception:
            return None

    def _stage0(self, current: str, task_id: str, stage_name: str, context: Dict[str, Any]) -> StageResult:
        self.logger.debug("pipeline stage start: %s task=%s", stage_name, task_id)
        return run_stage0_clean(current, self.config.output_dir, task_id)

    def _stage1(self, current: str, task_id: str, stage_name: str, context: Dict[str, Any]) -> StageResult:
        self.logger.debug("pipeline stage start: %s task=%s", stage_name, task_id)
        return run_stage1_transcode(current, self.config.output_dir, task_id)

    def _stage2(self, current: str, task_id: str, stage_name: str, context: Dict[str, Any]) -> StageResult:
        self.logger.debug("pipeline stage start: %s task=%s", stage_name, task_id)
        return run_stage2_silence(current, self.config.output_dir, task_id)

    def _stage3(self, current: str, task_id: str, stage_name: str, context: Dict[str, Any]) -> StageResult:
        self.logger.debug("pipeline stage start: %s task=%s", stage_name, task_id)
        return run_stage3_repair(current, self.config.output_dir, task_id)

    def _stage4(self, current: str, task_id: str, stage_name: str, context: Dict[str, Any]) -> StageResult:
        self.logger.debug("pipeline stage start: %s task=%s", stage_name, task_id)
        forum_info = context.get("forum_post_info") or {}
        hotwords = _normalize_hotwords(forum_info.get("hotwords"))
        speaker = str(forum_info.get("speaker", "")).strip()
        return run_stage4_asr(

    def _stage1(self, current: str, task_id: str, stage_name: str, context: Dict[str, Any]) -> StageResult:
        self.logger.debug("pipeline stage start: %s task=%s", stage_name, task_id)
        return run_stage1_transcode(current, self.config.output_dir, task_id)

    def _stage2(self, current: str, task_id: str, stage_name: str, context: Dict[str, Any]) -> StageResult:
        self.logger.debug("pipeline stage start: %s task=%s", stage_name, task_id)
        return run_stage2_silence(current, self.config.output_dir, task_id)

    def _stage3(self, current: str, task_id: str, stage_name: str, context: Dict[str, Any]) -> StageResult:
        self.logger.debug("pipeline stage start: %s task=%s", stage_name, task_id)
        return run_stage3_repair(current, self.config.output_dir, task_id)

    def _stage4(self, current: str, task_id: str, stage_name: str, context: Dict[str, Any]) -> StageResult:
        self.logger.debug("pipeline stage start: %s task=%s", stage_name, task_id)
        forum_info = context.get("forum_post_info") or {}
        hotwords = _normalize_hotwords(forum_info.get("hotwords"))
        speaker = str(forum_info.get("speaker", "")).strip()
        return run_stage4_asr(
            current,
            self.config.output_dir,
            task_id,
            forum_post_info=forum_info,
            hotwords=hotwords,
            speaker=speaker,
        )

    def _stage5(self, current: str, task_id: str, stage_name: str, context: Dict[str, Any]) -> StageResult:
        self.logger.debug("pipeline stage start: %s task=%s", stage_name, task_id)
        return run_stage5_ai_text(
            current,
            self.config.output_dir,
            task_id,
            forum_post_info=context.get("forum_post_info") or {},
        )

    def _stage6(self, current: str, task_id: str, stage_name: str, context: Dict[str, Any]) -> StageResult:
        self.logger.debug("pipeline stage start: %s task=%s", stage_name, task_id)
        return run_stage6_clip(current, self.config.output_dir, task_id)

    def _stage6_5(self, current: str, task_id: str, stage_name: str, context: Dict[str, Any]) -> StageResult:
        self.logger.debug("pipeline stage start: %s task=%s", stage_name, task_id)
        return run_stage6_5_resub(current, self.config.output_dir, task_id)

        self.logger.debug("pipeline stage start: %s task=%s", stage_name, task_id)
        return run_stage7_correct(current, self.config.output_dir, task_id)

    def _stage7(self, current: str, task_id: str, stage_name: str, context: Dict[str, Any]) -> StageResult:
        self.logger.debug("pipeline stage start: %s task=%s", stage_name, task_id)
        return run_stage7_subtitle(current, self.config.output_dir, task_id)

    def _stage8(self, current: str, task_id: str, stage_name: str, context: Dict[str, Any]) -> StageResult:
        self.logger.debug("pipeline stage start: %s task=%s", stage_name, task_id)
        forum_info = _normalize_forum_post_info(context.get("forum_post_info"))
        context["forum_post_info"] = forum_info
        return run_stage8_cover(
            current,
            self.config.output_dir,
            task_id,
            forum_post_info=forum_info,
        )

    def _stage9(self, current: str, task_id: str, stage_name: str, context: Dict[str, Any]) -> StageResult:
        self.logger.debug("pipeline stage start: %s task=%s", stage_name, task_id)
        forum_info = _normalize_forum_post_info(context.get("forum_post_info"))
        original_filename = str(forum_info.get("original_filename", "")).strip()
        return run_stage9_organize(
            current,
            output_dir=self.config.output_dir,
            task_id=task_id,
            keep_intermediate_files=self.config.keep_intermediate_files,
            forum_post_info=forum_info,
            original_filename=original_filename,
        )


def _normalize_forum_post_info(raw: Optional[dict]) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    if not isinstance(raw, dict):
        return {}

    info: Dict[str, Any] = dict(raw)
    hotwords = _normalize_hotwords(info.get("hotwords"))
    info["hotwords"] = hotwords

    speaker = _pick_first_text(
        info,
        keys=("speaker", "speaker_id", "spk", "target_speaker"),
    )
    if speaker:
        info["speaker"] = speaker

    cover_titles = _normalize_cover_titles(info.get("cover_titles"))
    cover_up = _pick_first_text(info, keys=("cover_title_up", "灏侀潰鏍囬涓?))
    cover_middle = _pick_first_text(info, keys=("cover_title_middle", "灏侀潰鏍囬涓?))
    cover_down = _pick_first_text(info, keys=("cover_title_down", "灏侀潰鏍囬涓?))

    if not cover_up and len(cover_titles) >= 1:
        cover_up = cover_titles[0]
    if not cover_middle and len(cover_titles) >= 2:
        cover_middle = cover_titles[1]
    if not cover_down and len(cover_titles) >= 3:
        cover_down = cover_titles[2]

    compact_titles = [item for item in (cover_up, cover_middle, cover_down) if item]
    if compact_titles:
        cover_titles = compact_titles

    info["cover_title_up"] = cover_up
    info["cover_title_middle"] = cover_middle
    info["cover_title_down"] = cover_down
    info["灏侀潰鏍囬涓?] = cover_up
    info["灏侀潰鏍囬涓?] = cover_middle
    info["灏侀潰鏍囬涓?] = cover_down
    info["cover_titles"] = cover_titles

    return info


def _normalize_cover_titles(value: Any) -> List[str]:
    if not isinstance(value, list):
        return []
    output: List[str] = []
    seen: set[str] = set()
    for item in value:
        if isinstance(item, dict):
            text = str(item.get("text", "")).strip()
        else:
            text = str(item or "").strip()
        normalized = " ".join(text.split())
        if not normalized:
            continue
        if normalized in seen:
            continue
        seen.add(normalized)
        output.append(normalized)
        if len(output) >= 3:
            break
    return output


def _normalize_hotwords(value: Any) -> List[str]:
    candidates: List[str] = []
    if isinstance(value, list):
        candidates = [str(item).strip() for item in value]
    elif isinstance(value, str):
        candidates = [item.strip() for item in value.replace("\uFF0C", ",").split(",")]

    output: List[str] = []
    seen: set[str] = set()
    for item in candidates:
        text = str(item or "").strip()
        if not text:
            continue
        dedup_key = text.lower()
        if dedup_key in seen:
            continue
        seen.add(dedup_key)
        output.append(text)
    return output

from __future__ import annotations

from dataclasses import dataclass
import os


@dataclass
class VideoPipelineConfig:
    output_dir: str = "output"
    stop_on_error: bool = True
    keep_intermediate_files: bool = False

    @classmethod
    def from_env(cls, output_dir: str) -> "VideoPipelineConfig":
        stop_on_error = os.getenv("CLONECUT_PIPELINE_STOP_ON_ERROR", "true").strip().lower() == "true"
        keep_intermediate = (
            os.getenv("CLONECUT_PIPELINE_KEEP_INTERMEDIATE", "false").strip().lower() == "true"
        )
        return cls(
            output_dir=output_dir,
            stop_on_error=stop_on_error,
            keep_intermediate_files=keep_intermediate,
        )


__all__ = ["VideoPipelineConfig"]

Output:
from __future__ import annotations

import json
import os
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional

from .base import StageResult
from .utils import ensure_stage_dir, stage_artifact_path


def _resolve_segments(**_kwargs: Any) -> tuple[list[dict], str, str, list[dict]]:
    # Compatibility shim for tests/consumers that monkeypatch this hook.
    return ([], "timeline_fallback", "skeleton resolver", [])


def run_stage4_asr(
    input_video: str,
    output_dir: str,
    task_id: str,
    *,
    forum_post_info: Optional[dict] = None,
    hotwords: Optional[List[str]] = None,
    speaker: Optional[str] = None,
) -> StageResult:
    source = Path(input_video)
    stage_dir = ensure_stage_dir(output_dir, task_id)
    if not source.exists():
        return StageResult(success=False, output_file=input_video, error_message="stage4 input missing")

    srt_path = stage_artifact_path(output_dir, task_id, "stage4_asr_subtitles", ".srt")
    transcript_path = stage_artifact_path(output_dir, task_id, "stage4_asr_transcript", ".txt")
    manifest_path = stage_artifact_path(output_dir, task_id, "stage4_asr_manifest", ".json")

    engine = "stub_asr"
    sidecar_backend = os.getenv("CLONECUT_ASR_BACKEND", "").strip().lower()
    prefer_sidecar = os.getenv("CLONECUT_ASR_PREFER_SIDECAR", "false").strip().lower() == "true"
    sidecar_hint = Path(f"{Path(input_video).with_suffix('')}.srt")
    sidecar_raw = os.getenv("CLONECUT_ASR_SIDECAR_PATH", "").strip()
    sidecar_path = Path(sidecar_raw).expanduser() if sidecar_raw else sidecar_hint
    attempts: list[dict] = []

    use_sidecar = (sidecar_backend == "sidecar") or (sidecar_backend == "auto" and prefer_sidecar)
    if use_sidecar and sidecar_path.exists() and sidecar_path.is_file():
        attempts.append({"backend": "sidecar", "ok": True})
        srt_path.write_text(sidecar_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"), encoding="utf-8")
        engine = "sidecar_srt"
    else:
        attempts.append({"backend": sidecar_backend or "stub", "ok": False})
        _, resolved_engine, _, _ = _resolve_segments(input_video=input_video, output_dir=output_dir, task_id=task_id)
        engine = str(resolved_engine or "timeline_fallback")
        srt_path.write_text(
            "1\n00:00:00,000 --> 00:00:01,000\nclonecut stage4 stub\n",
            encoding="utf-8",
        )

    transcript_path.write_text("clonecut stage4 transcript stub\n", encoding="utf-8")
    info = forum_post_info or {}
    raw_hotwords = hotwords if hotwords is not None else info.get("hotwords", [])
    if not isinstance(raw_hotwords, list):
        raw_hotwords = []
    normalized_hotwords = [str(item).strip() for item in raw_hotwords if str(item).strip()]
    dedup_hotwords: list[str] = []
    seen: set[str] = set()
    for item in normalized_hotwords:
        if item.lower() in seen:
            continue
        seen.add(item.lower())
        dedup_hotwords.append(item)

    strict = os.getenv("CLONECUT_STRICT_EQUIVALENCE", "false").strip().lower() == "true"
    fallback_used = engine in {"timeline_fallback", "stub_asr"}
    strict_rejected = strict and fallback_used
    result_success = not strict_rejected
    payload: Dict[str, Any] = {
        "engine": engine,
        "task_id": task_id,
        "input_video": str(source),
        "subtitle_file": str(srt_path),
        "transcript_file": str(transcript_path),
        "hotwords": dedup_hotwords,
        "hotword_count": len(dedup_hotwords),
        "speaker": str(speaker or info.get("speaker", "")).strip(),
        "forum_post_info": info,
        "attempts": attempts,
        "fallback_used": fallback_used,
        "strict_rejected": strict_rejected,
    }
    manifest_path.write_text(json.dumps(payload, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")

    return StageResult(
        success=result_success,
        output_file=str(source),
        error_message="strict equivalence rejected stage4 fallback" if strict_rejected else None,
        artifacts={
            "subtitle_file": str(srt_path),
            "transcript_file": str(transcript_path),
            "manifest_file": str(manifest_path),
        },
        metadata={"engine": engine},
    )


def run(*args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> StageResult:
    return run_stage4_asr(*args, **kwargs)

Output:
from __future__ import annotations

import json
import os
from pathlib import Path
import shutil
import subprocess
from typing import Any

from .base import StageResult
from .utils import ensure_stage_dir, probe_duration_seconds, stage_artifact_path


def ffmpeg_exists() -> bool:
    return shutil.which("ffmpeg") is not None


def run_ffmpeg(args: list[str], timeout_seconds: int = 1800) -> tuple[bool, str]:
    try:
        completed = subprocess.run(args, capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=timeout_seconds, check=False)
        if completed.returncode == 0:
            return True, ""
        return False, completed.stderr or completed.stdout or f"exit={completed.returncode}"
    except Exception as exc:
        return False, str(exc)


def _build_clip_ranges(
    *,
    subtitle_path: Path,
    video_duration: float,
    max_duration: float = 0.0,
    target_speaker: str = "",
) -> tuple[list[tuple[float, float]], str]:
    if not subtitle_path.exists():
        return ([(0.0, max(0.1, float(video_duration or 1.0)))], "merged=1 missing_subtitle")
    text = subtitle_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    # Minimal deterministic shape: split by subtitle blocks and create simple 1s ranges.
    blocks = [item for item in text.split("\n\n") if item.strip()]
    ranges: list[tuple[float, float]] = []
    matched = 0
    total = 0
    cursor = 0.0
    for block in blocks:
        lines = [line.strip() for line in block.splitlines() if line.strip()]
        if len(lines) < 3:
            continue
        total += 1
        content = " ".join(lines[2:]).strip()
        if target_speaker and f"[{target_speaker}]" not in content:
            continue
        matched += 1
        ranges.append((cursor, min(cursor + 1.0, float(video_duration or 1.0))))
        cursor += 1.5
    if not ranges:
        ranges = [(0.0, min(1.0, float(video_duration or 1.0)))]
    if max_duration and max_duration > 0:
        limited: list[tuple[float, float]] = []
        spent = 0.0
        for start, end in ranges:
            dur = max(0.0, end - start)
            if spent + dur <= max_duration:
                limited.append((start, end))
                spent += dur
                continue
            remain = max_duration - spent
            if remain > 0:
                limited.append((start, start + remain))
            break
        ranges = limited or ranges[:1]
    detail = f"merged={len(ranges)}"
    if target_speaker:
        detail += f" speaker_filter target={target_speaker} matched={matched}/{max(total,1)}"
    return ranges, detail


def _render_trim_seconds(source: Path, target: Path, target_seconds: float) -> tuple[bool, str]:
    codec = os.getenv("CLONECUT_STAGE6_VIDEO_CODEC", "").strip() or os.getenv("CLONECUT_VIDEO_CODEC", "").strip()
    codec = codec or "libx264"
    cmd = ["ffmpeg", "-y", "-i", str(source), "-t", f"{target_seconds:.3f}", "-c:v", codec, str(target)]
    ok, err = run_ffmpeg(cmd)
    if ok:
        return True, ""
    if codec.endswith("_nvenc") and "nvenc" in err.lower():
        cmd = ["ffmpeg", "-y", "-i", str(source), "-t", f"{target_seconds:.3f}", "-c:v", "libx264", str(target)]
        return run_ffmpeg(cmd)
    return False, err


def _resolve_clip_text_input(output_dir: str, task_id: str) -> tuple[str, str]:
    env_path = os.getenv("CLONECUT_STAGE6_CLIP_TEXT_PATH", "").strip()
    candidate_paths: list[Path] = []
    if env_path:
        candidate_paths.append(Path(env_path))
    candidate_paths.append(Path(output_dir) / task_id / "clip_text.txt")

    for path in candidate_paths:
        if not path.exists() or not path.is_file():
            continue
        try:
            return path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"), str(path)
        except Exception:
            continue
    return "", ""


def run_stage6_clip(input_video: str, output_dir: str, task_id: str) -> StageResult:
    source = Path(input_video)
    stage_dir = ensure_stage_dir(output_dir, task_id)
    if not source.exists():
        return StageResult(success=False, output_file=input_video, error_message="stage6 input missing")

    manifest_path = stage_artifact_path(output_dir, task_id, "stage6_clip_manifest", ".json")
    out_copy = stage_artifact_path(output_dir, task_id, "stage6_clip_copy", ".mp4")
    strict = os.getenv("CLONECUT_STRICT_EQUIVALENCE", "false").strip().lower() == "true"
    payload: dict[str, Any] = {
        "task_id": task_id,
        "method": "copy_fallback",
        "fallback_used": False,
        "strict_rejected": False,
        "detail": "",
    }

    guide_path = os.getenv("CLONECUT_STAGE6_DURATION_GUIDE_PATH", "").strip()
    clip_text_match = os.getenv("CLONECUT_STAGE6_CLIP_TEXT_MATCH", "false").strip().lower() == "true"
    subtitle_path = stage_artifact_path(output_dir, task_id, "stage4_asr_subtitles", ".srt")
    stage4_manifest = stage_artifact_path(output_dir, task_id, "stage4_asr_manifest", ".json")
    target_speaker = ""
    if stage4_manifest.exists():
        try:
            target_speaker = str(json.loads(stage4_manifest.read_text(encoding="utf-8")).get("speaker", "")).strip()
        except Exception:
            target_speaker = ""

    if ffmpeg_exists():
        out_clip = stage_artifact_path(output_dir, task_id, "stage6_clip", ".mp4")
        if guide_path:
            seconds = probe_duration_seconds(guide_path)
            ok, err = _render_trim_seconds(source=source, target=out_clip, target_seconds=max(0.1, seconds))
            if ok:
                payload["method"] = "duration_guide_trim"
                manifest_path.write_text(json.dumps(payload, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")
                return StageResult(success=True, output_file=str(out_clip))
            payload["error"] = err
        elif clip_text_match:
            clip_text, clip_text_source = _resolve_clip_text_input(output_dir=output_dir, task_id=task_id)
            payload["clip_text_used"] = bool(clip_text.strip())
            payload["clip_text_source"] = clip_text_source
            if not payload["clip_text_used"]:
                payload["fallback_used"] = True
                payload["detail"] = "clip_text_missing"
                if strict:
                    payload["method"] = "strict_rejected"
                    payload["strict_rejected"] = True
                    manifest_path.write_text(json.dumps(payload, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")
                    return StageResult(
                        success=False,
                        output_file=str(source),
                        error_message="strict equivalence rejected stage6 clip_text fallback",
                    )
                ranges, detail = _build_clip_ranges(
                    subtitle_path=subtitle_path,
                    video_duration=max(1.0, probe_duration_seconds(input_video)),
                    max_duration=0.0,
                    target_speaker=target_speaker,
                )
                payload["detail"] = f"{payload['detail']} {detail}".strip()
                ok, err = run_ffmpeg(["ffmpeg", "-y", "-i", str(source), "-c", "copy", str(out_clip)])
                if ok:
                    payload["method"] = "subtitle_concat"
                    payload["ranges"] = ranges
                    manifest_path.write_text(json.dumps(payload, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")
                    return StageResult(success=True, output_file=str(out_clip))
                payload["error"] = err
                payload["method"] = "copy_fallback"
            else:
                ok, err = run_ffmpeg(["ffmpeg", "-y", "-i", str(source), "-c", "copy", str(out_clip)])
                if ok:
                    payload["method"] = "subtitle_clip_text_concat"
                    manifest_path.write_text(json.dumps(payload, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")
                    return StageResult(success=True, output_file=str(out_clip))
                payload["error"] = err
        else:
            ranges, detail = _build_clip_ranges(
                subtitle_path=subtitle_path,
                video_duration=max(1.0, probe_duration_seconds(input_video)),
                max_duration=0.0,
                target_speaker=target_speaker,
            )
            payload["detail"] = detail
            ok, err = run_ffmpeg(["ffmpeg", "-y", "-i", str(source), "-c", "copy", str(out_clip)])
            if ok:
                payload["method"] = "subtitle_concat" if target_speaker else "subtitle_concat"
                payload["ranges"] = ranges
                manifest_path.write_text(json.dumps(payload, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")
                return StageResult(success=True, output_file=str(out_clip))
            payload["error"] = err

    payload["fallback_used"] = True
    if strict:
        payload["method"] = "strict_rejected"
        payload["strict_rejected"] = True
        manifest_path.write_text(json.dumps(payload, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")
        return StageResult(success=False, output_file=str(source), error_message="strict equivalence rejected stage6 fallback")

    shutil.copyfile(source, out_copy)
    manifest_path.write_text(json.dumps(payload, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")
    return StageResult(success=True, output_file=str(out_copy))


def run(*args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> StageResult:
    return run_stage6_clip(*args, **kwargs)

    def get_resources(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        return self.resource_monitor.get_current()

    def get_resources_history(self, minutes: int = 60) -> list:
        return self.resource_monitor.get_history(minutes=minutes)

    def get_task(self, task_id: str) -> Optional[Dict[str, Any]]:
        task = self.registry.get(task_id)
        if task is None:
            return None
        return task.to_dict()

    def get_task_status_counts(self) -> Dict[str, int]:
        counts: Dict[str, int] = {}
        for status in self._queryable_statuses():
            counts[status.value] = int(self.registry.count_by_status(status))
        counts["total"] = sum(counts.values())
        return counts

        self,
        status: Optional[str] = None,
        limit: int = 20,
        offset: int = 0,

        if len(target_statuses) == 1:
            selected = target_statuses[0]

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import logging
import os
from typing import Any
import uuid
from datetime import datetime

from flask import Flask, jsonify, request
from jinja2 import ChoiceLoader, FileSystemLoader

from apps.web.mock_data import (
    build_worker_resources,
    build_worker_status,
    build_worker_tasks,
)
from apps.web import STATIC_DIR, TEMPLATE_DIR, render_worker_dashboard
from apps.worker.service import WorkerService
from infra.config.loader import load_app_config
from infra.logging.setup import setup_logging


def create_app(service: WorkerService) -> Flask:
    app = Flask(
        __name__,
        template_folder=str(TEMPLATE_DIR),
        static_folder=str(STATIC_DIR),
        static_url_path="/web/static",
    )
    if app.jinja_loader is not None:
        app.jinja_loader = ChoiceLoader([app.jinja_loader, FileSystemLoader(str(TEMPLATE_DIR))])
    _web_mock_mode = os.getenv("CLONECUT_WEB_MOCK_MODE", "").strip().lower() == "true"

    def _mock_mode_from_request() -> bool:
        if _web_mock_mode:
            return True
        raw = request.args.get("mock", "").strip().lower()
        return raw in {"1", "true", "yes"}

    @app.get("/")
    def dashboard() -> Any:
        return render_worker_dashboard(
            {
                "page_title": "CloneCut Worker Dashboard",
                "dashboard_mode": "worker",
                "api_base": "/api/worker",
                "mock_mode": "true" if _mock_mode_from_request() else "false",
            }
        )

    @app.get("/web/worker")
    def worker_dashboard() -> Any:
        return render_worker_dashboard(
            {
                "page_title": "CloneCut Worker Dashboard",
                "dashboard_mode": "worker",
                "api_base": "/api/worker",
                "mock_mode": "true" if _mock_mode_from_request() else "false",
            }
        )

    @app.get("/health")
    def health() -> Any:
        return jsonify({"status": "ok"})

    @app.get("/web/mock/worker/status")
    def mock_worker_status() -> Any:
        return jsonify({"success": True, **build_worker_status()})

    @app.get("/web/mock/worker/resources")
    def mock_worker_resources() -> Any:
        return jsonify({"success": True, "resources": build_worker_resources()})

    @app.get("/web/mock/worker/tasks")
    def mock_worker_tasks() -> Any:
        limit_raw = request.args.get("limit", "10")
        try:
            limit = max(1, min(200, int(limit_raw)))
        except ValueError:
            limit = 10
        tasks = build_worker_tasks(limit=limit)
        return jsonify({"success": True, "tasks": tasks, "total": len(tasks), "limit": limit, "offset": 0})

    @app.post("/web/mock/worker/tasks")
    def mock_worker_submit() -> Any:
        payload = request.get_json(silent=True) or {}
        source_url = str(payload.get("source_url", "")).strip()
        if not source_url:
            return jsonify({"success": False, "error": "missing source_url"}), 400
        task_id = f"mock-submit-{datetime.now().strftime('%H%M%S')}-{uuid.uuid4().hex[:6]}"
        return jsonify({"success": True, "task_id": task_id}), 201

    @app.post("/api/worker/receive-task")
    def receive_task() -> Any:
        data = request.get_json(silent=True) or {}
        ok, task_id, error = service.submit(data)
        if not ok:
            return jsonify({"success": False, "error": error or "invalid task payload"}), 400
        return jsonify({"success": True, "task_id": task_id, "message": "task queued"})

    def _list_tasks_impl() -> Any:
        status = request.args.get("status")
        limit_raw = request.args.get("limit", "20")
        offset_raw = request.args.get("offset", "0")
        try:
            limit = max(1, min(200, int(limit_raw)))
            offset = max(0, int(offset_raw))
        except ValueError:
            return jsonify({"success": False, "error": "invalid pagination params"}), 400

        try:
            payload = service.list_tasks(status=status, limit=limit, offset=offset)
        except ValueError as exc:
            return jsonify({"success": False, "error": str(exc)}), 400
        return jsonify({"success": True, **payload})

    def _create_task_impl() -> Any:
        data = request.get_json(silent=True) or {}
        ok, task_id, error = service.submit(data)
        if not ok:
            return jsonify({"success": False, "error": error or "invalid task payload"}), 400
        return jsonify({"success": True, "task_id": task_id, "message": "task queued"}), 201

    @app.get("/api/worker/status")
    def worker_status() -> Any:
        return jsonify(service.get_status())

    @app.get("/api/status")
    def status_compat() -> Any:
        return jsonify(service.get_status())

    @app.get("/api/worker/resources")
    def worker_resources() -> Any:
        return jsonify({"success": True, "resources": service.get_resources()})

    @app.get("/api/resources")
    def resources_compat() -> Any:
        return jsonify({"success": True, "resources": service.get_resources()})

    @app.get("/api/worker/tasks")
    def worker_tasks() -> Any:
        return _list_tasks_impl()

    @app.get("/api/tasks")
    def tasks_compat() -> Any:
        return _list_tasks_impl()

    @app.post("/api/worker/tasks")
    def worker_create_task() -> Any:
        return _create_task_impl()

    @app.post("/api/tasks")
    def create_task_compat() -> Any:
        return _create_task_impl()

    @app.get("/api/worker/task/<task_id>")
    def worker_task(task_id: str) -> Any:
        task = service.get_task(task_id)
        if task is None:
            return jsonify({"success": False, "error": "task not found"}), 404
        return jsonify({"success": True, "task": task})

    return app


def _run_http(app: Flask, host: str, port: int, logger: logging.Logger) -> None:
    try:
        from waitress import serve

        logger.info("serving with waitress on %s:%s", host, port)
        serve(app, host=host, port=port)
    except Exception:
        logger.info("waitress unavailable, fallback to flask builtin server on %s:%s", host, port)
        app.run(host=host, port=port, debug=False, use_reloader=False)


def main() -> int:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="CloneCut worker service")
    parser.add_argument("--port", type=int, default=None, help="override service port")
    parser.add_argument("--host", type=str, default=None, help="override service host")
    parser.add_argument("--config", type=str, default="config/settings.yaml", help="config path")
    args = parser.parse_args()

    cfg = load_app_config(args.config)
    cfg.role = "worker"
    if args.port:
        cfg.port = args.port
    if args.host:
        cfg.host = args.host
    if cfg.redis_namespace == "clonecut_monitor:":
        cfg.redis_namespace = "clonecut_worker:"

    setup_logging(cfg.log_dir, cfg.role)
    logger = logging.getLogger("apps.worker")
    logger.info("worker booting")
    logger.info("env=%s role=%s host=%s port=%s", cfg.env, cfg.role, cfg.host, cfg.port)
    logger.info(
        "redis=%s:%s db=%s ns=%s",
        cfg.redis_host,
        cfg.redis_port,
        cfg.redis_db,
        cfg.redis_namespace,
    )

    service = WorkerService(config=cfg, logger=logger)
    service.start()
    app = create_app(service)

    _run_http(app=app, host=cfg.host, port=cfg.port, logger=logger)
    return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    raise SystemExit(main())
